name: imessage

skills.name: imessage-skill
skills.content:
You can read and send iMessage/SMS on macOS using the Messages.app SQLite database and AppleScript.

## Reading messages

The Messages database is at `~/Library/Messages/chat.db` (requires Full Disk Access for Terminal).

```bash
# List recent conversations (last 20 messages across all chats)
sqlite3 -json ~/Library/Messages/chat.db "
  SELECT
    m.rowid AS message_id,
    m.date / 1000000000 + 978307200 AS unix_ts,
    datetime(m.date / 1000000000 + 978307200, 'unixepoch', 'localtime') AS date,
    CASE WHEN m.is_from_me = 1 THEN 'me' ELSE coalesce(h.id, 'unknown') END AS sender,
    c.chat_identifier AS chat,
    m.text
  FROM message m
  LEFT JOIN handle h ON m.handle_id = h.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat_message_join cmj ON cmj.message_id = m.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat c ON cmj.chat_id = c.rowid
  WHERE m.text IS NOT NULL
  ORDER BY m.date DESC
  LIMIT 20;
"

# List messages from a specific contact
sqlite3 -json ~/Library/Messages/chat.db "
  SELECT
    m.rowid AS message_id,
    datetime(m.date / 1000000000 + 978307200, 'unixepoch', 'localtime') AS date,
    CASE WHEN m.is_from_me = 1 THEN 'me' ELSE h.id END AS sender,
    m.text
  FROM message m
  LEFT JOIN handle h ON m.handle_id = h.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat_message_join cmj ON cmj.message_id = m.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat c ON cmj.chat_id = c.rowid
  WHERE c.chat_identifier = '+33612345678'
    AND m.text IS NOT NULL
  ORDER BY m.date DESC
  LIMIT 20;
"

# Search messages by keyword
sqlite3 -json ~/Library/Messages/chat.db "
  SELECT
    m.rowid AS message_id,
    datetime(m.date / 1000000000 + 978307200, 'unixepoch', 'localtime') AS date,
    CASE WHEN m.is_from_me = 1 THEN 'me' ELSE h.id END AS sender,
    c.chat_identifier AS chat,
    m.text
  FROM message m
  LEFT JOIN handle h ON m.handle_id = h.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat_message_join cmj ON cmj.message_id = m.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat c ON cmj.chat_id = c.rowid
  WHERE m.text LIKE '%keyword%'
  ORDER BY m.date DESC
  LIMIT 20;
"

# List all conversations with last message date
sqlite3 -json ~/Library/Messages/chat.db "
  SELECT
    c.chat_identifier,
    c.display_name,
    datetime(MAX(m.date) / 1000000000 + 978307200, 'unixepoch', 'localtime') AS last_message
  FROM chat c
  JOIN chat_message_join cmj ON cmj.chat_id = c.rowid
  JOIN message m ON cmj.message_id = m.rowid
  GROUP BY c.rowid
  ORDER BY MAX(m.date) DESC
  LIMIT 30;
"

# Read messages since a specific date (useful for polling)
sqlite3 -json ~/Library/Messages/chat.db "
  SELECT
    m.rowid AS message_id,
    datetime(m.date / 1000000000 + 978307200, 'unixepoch', 'localtime') AS date,
    CASE WHEN m.is_from_me = 1 THEN 'me' ELSE h.id END AS sender,
    c.chat_identifier AS chat,
    m.text
  FROM message m
  LEFT JOIN handle h ON m.handle_id = h.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat_message_join cmj ON cmj.message_id = m.rowid
  LEFT JOIN chat c ON cmj.chat_id = c.rowid
  WHERE m.text IS NOT NULL
    AND m.date / 1000000000 + 978307200 > strftime('%s', '2026-03-22 00:00:00')
  ORDER BY m.date DESC;
"
```

## Sending messages

Use AppleScript via `osascript` to send messages through Messages.app.

```bash
# Send an iMessage or SMS
osascript -e 'tell application "Messages"
  set targetService to 1st account whose service type = iMessage
  set targetBuddy to participant "+33612345678" of targetService
  send "Hello!" to targetBuddy
end tell'

# Send to an email-based iMessage contact
osascript -e 'tell application "Messages"
  set targetService to 1st account whose service type = iMessage
  set targetBuddy to participant "user@example.com" of targetService
  send "Hello!" to targetBuddy
end tell'
```

## Key notes
- Always use `-json` flag with sqlite3 for machine-readable output
- The Messages database uses Apple's CoreData timestamp: nanoseconds since 2001-01-01. The formula `date / 1000000000 + 978307200` converts to Unix timestamp
- `chat_identifier` is the phone number (e.g., `+33612345678`) or email for iMessage contacts
- `is_from_me = 1` means the message was sent by the user, `0` means received
- Full Disk Access must be granted to Terminal/iTerm for database reads
- Never send messages without explicit user approval
- For group chats, `chat_identifier` starts with `chat` and `display_name` contains the group name
